channels.dialogs.status.enable.title: Enable Channel
channels.dialogs.status.enable.descripti: Are you sure you want to enable channel "{{name}}"? The channel will be available for use after enabling.
channels.dialogs.status.enable.warning: Please ensure the channel configuration is correct and test the connection before enabling to avoid service interruptions.

channels.dialogs.status.archive.descript: Are you sure you want to archive channel "{{name}}"? The channel will be unavailable after archiving.
channels.dialogs.status.archive.warning: Archived channels will be hidden from the default list. You can still find them by filtering by archived status.
channels.dialogs.status.restore.title: Restore Channel
channels.dialogs.status.restore.descript: Are you sure you want to restore channel "{{name}}"? The channel will be available for use after restoring.
channels.dialogs.status.restore.info: Restored channels will reappear in the default list.
channels.dialogs.settings.extraModelPref: Configure extra model prefixes to allow access to this channel using prefixed model names. For example: with prefix 'deepseek', both 'deepseek/deepseek-chat' and 'deepseek-chat' can access this channel.
channels.dialogs.settings.autoTrimedMode: Configure prefixes to automatically trim from model names. For example: with prefix 'deepseek-ai', model 'deepseek-ai/deepseek-chat' becomes 'deepseek-chat'; both 'deepseek-ai/deepseek-chat' and 'deepseek-chat' can access this channel
No prefixes found in supported models
All prefixes have already been added
channels.dialogs.settings.modelMapping.d: Use aliases to expose channel-supported models under alternative names without transforming the request model. For example: register 'gpt-4' as an alias of 'gpt-4-turbo', and both names will be handled by this channel
channels.dialogs.settings.modelMapping.v: Both alias and target model are required
channels.dialogs.settings.modelMapping.t: Target model must be in supported models
channels.dialogs.settings.modelMapping.d: Each original model can only be mapped once
channels.dialogs.settings.modelMapping.h: When enabled, only the transformed model names (aliases, prefixed models, etc.) will be exposed in the model list, hiding all untransformed original model names.
channels.dialogs.settings.overrides.body: Override request body send to the channel, you can change the body content, add, delete, or rename fields.
channels.dialogs.settings.overrides.head: Override Headers
Configure custom HTTP headers that will be applied to all requests sent through this channel.
Key
e.g. X-Custom-Header
Value
Header value
From
e.g. X-Old-Header
To
e.g. X-New-Header
This header may contain authentication credentials. Double-check before saving.
channels.dialogs.settings.overrides.vali: Operation {{index}} ({{op}}): Both source and target paths are required
Header operation {{index}} ({{op}}): Key cannot be empty
Header operation {{index}} ({{op}}): Both source and target keys are required

channels.dialogs.bulkDisable.title: Bulk Disable Channels
channels.dialogs.bulkDisable.description: Are you sure you want to disable {{count}} selected channels? Disabled channels won't process requests until re-enabled.

channels.dialogs.bulkEnable.description: Are you sure you want to enable {{count}} selected channels? Enabled channels will be available to process requests.
channels.dialogs.bulkEnable.warning: This action will make all selected channels available for use. Make sure the channels are properly configured.

channels.dialogs.bulkTest.description: Run test requests for {{count}} selected channels and quickly re-enable the recovered ones.

channels.dialogs.bulkTest.noRecoverableC: No recovered channels can be enabled yet.
